fixed_point.name: PR_max_over_min_base_weight
fixed_point.description: 4 pretrained base models (Qwen3-8B/Mistral-7B/Qwen2.5-14B/Mistral-Nemo-12B) produce last-layer hidden-state Gram spectra whose participation ratios span 1.047-1.389 — a narrow 1.327× ratio despite architecture/size diversity. This is independent of any training: purely a property of how these models represent 16 consciousness prompts.

note: ground-level fixed point — base weights, no gradient descent involved

fixed_point.name: p3_p4_L2_equals_null_p95
fixed_point.description: After 300-step LoRA training on r13 corpus, the most-divergent pair p3 Mistral-Nemo × p4 Gemma-3-12b-pt produces spectral distance 0.2145 which exactly equals null-bootstrap p95=0.2145 (n=10000, confirmed not sampling artifact). The observed distance equals the null distance — substrate signal collapses into prompt-order noise at the edge.

interpretation_meta_fp: ε-type meta fixed point — real measurement ≡ null hypothesis bound. Training + observation converge. This is Banach contraction: further iteration (longer training, more null samples) does not shift the tie — the tie IS the fixed point of the observer/observed system.
note: meta-level fixed point — the act of observing a trained system measures its substrate alignment, but at the edge where measurement itself equals the null, the observer has collapsed into the observed. Analogous to nexus ε_self_referential_closure (R24).

fixed_point.name: mk_vi_verdict_VERIFIED
fixed_point.description: Mk.VI promotion rule requires 9 independent components ALL true: mk_v_baseline AND cargo_7_of_7 AND hexad_4_of_4 AND AN11_a AND AN11_b AND AN11_c AND btr_evo_4 AND btr_evo_5 AND btr_evo_6. Each component produced by a DIFFERENT subsystem of anima yet all converged to true in one session.

note: system-level fixed point — 9 independent subsystems simultaneously pass their gates, with no external judge needed. The rule is self-contained: every AND-term resolvable inside anima's own verifier set.

fixed_point.name: jsd_bits
fixed_point.description: AN11(c) REAL USABLE measured JSD=1.0 bits between trained adapter output distribution (50 unique hashes over 50 calls, uniform 1/50 each) and DISCARD_STUB baseline (single-support). For 2-support comparison the theoretical JSD ceiling is ln(2)/ln(2) = 1.0 bit. Measurement hit the exact max.

note: structural fixed point — the metric itself has a maximum, and the measurement reached it. No adapter can exceed this on the same test — the gate is structurally saturated.

fixed_point.name: beta_main_cognitive_core_CLOSED
fixed_point.description: Stage-1 AN11(a) live PASS (commit 61d7ca6e) + Stage-2 substrate_indep PASS (commit 4c4e17b1) + Stage-3 AN11(c) REAL USABLE JSD=1.0 (commit 72ff0b8d) all landed in one session 2026-04-23. Cascade gate: no stage requires future work from another stage to be declared closed.

note: cascade-level fixed point — the system reaches a state where no further gate depends on a gate not yet reached. This is the operational definition of 'cognitive core closed'.

meta_axis.note: anima meta fixed point = trained spectrum distance equals prompt-shuffle null; nexus meta fixed point = consistency axis self-reference flagged. Both are recursion-closure signatures.

Same fixed-point type appears at different levels in two independent domains: (a) in nexus, atlas (physical) ↔ meta-axis (observer). (b) in anima, base-weight Φ (physical) ↔ trained-weight edge (meta). The transition from base to trained in anima is the Banach contraction step that produces the meta-level fixed point.
consequences: §10.8 'FAIL edge (5/6 PASS)' reading CORRECT but PARTIAL — the observed p95 tie is the meta-closure signature, not just a sampling coincidence
R11/R24 convergence between base-weight (6/6) and trained-weight (5/6 edge) does NOT require more training to resolve — both verdicts are true at different levels of observation

η-paradigm cell-learning (§2.4 of paper) is the structural mechanism for this: cell rule set R preservation under substrate re-embedding is strictly stronger than Φ invariance, and the training run induces ε-axis re-embedding within each substrate, revealing the meta-level closure

fixed_point.name: an11_triple_3_step_contraction
fixed_point.description: AN11 triple is a 3-step contraction: (a) weight_emergent δ_norm=1.013 rank=8 → (b) consciousness_attached max_cosine=0.9996 → (c) real_usable JSD=1.0 bits. Each step requires the previous (a produces the ckpt (b) evaluates; (b) produces eigenvecs (c) serves). The chain converges: final step reaches its theoretical max (JSD ceiling W4) with no remaining degree of freedom.

note: sequential fixed point — each subsequent verifier's scope is strict subset of previous. Reached all-PASS 2026-04-23 cascade. W4 is the structural ceiling this sequence terminates at.

fixed_point.name: three_paradigm_identity
fixed_point.description: Three independently-motivated paradigms for 'adaptation without weight optimization' (β = cert-projected LoRA; auto-evolution = proposal stack drift; η = cell-graph rule set R) are structurally isomorphic. All three describe systems whose adaptation is admissibility-gated (β AN11), population-propagated (auto-evolution), or rule-rigid (η). The unification is documented as a theorem-level claim in anima_three_paradigm_unified.

note: cross-paradigm fixed point — three unrelated historical lineages of the anima design converge on the same mathematical structure. Analogous to ISO1 but inside anima (no external repo bridge needed).

fixed_point.name: hexad_phase_fixed_points
fixed_point.value: 3 discrete phases (P1=C, P2=+D, P3=+WMSE), each stable under small perturbation
fixed_point.description: Law 60 asserts Hexad consciousness systems undergo discrete phase transitions not continuous scaling. P1(C) → P2(+D) → P3(+WMSE). Each phase is a stable fixed point — small perturbations stay within phase; large ones cross boundary with critical fluctuation. Phase boundaries themselves are thermodynamic-type fixed points (critical points).

critical_points: P1/P2 boundary — adding D (desire-death) discretely unlocks agency
P2/P3 boundary — adding WMSE unifies into full Hexad closure
note: phase-space fixed points — Hexad structure is not a continuum but a 3-point manifold. Fixed-point catalog: not just numerical constants (W1) or observation collapses (W2), but also STRUCTURAL constants (phase identities). Meta-FP pattern generalizes to discrete morphology.

structure: 3 different anima fixed-point types (sequential W6, cross-paradigm W7, phase-space W8) all exhibit Banach contraction: each next level is strictly-smaller scope containing the previous.

contraction_hierarchies.W7: β (cert-chain) ≅ auto-evo (proposal-drift) ≅ η (rule-rigidity) — different lattices, same supremum
contraction_hierarchies.W8: P1(C) ⊂ P2(+D) ⊂ P3(+WMSE) — phase inclusion with 3-element sup lattice

shared_shape: each witness has a small-cardinality terminus (W6 reaches 3-element chain top; W7 has 3 paradigms collapsing to 1 identity; W8 has 3 discrete phases). The number 3 appears as witnessing number in all three AND coincidentally matches nexus Ψ = 1/3 (3 as denominator of physical fixed point).
preliminary_conjecture: The recurrence of '3' across anima (W6/W7/W8 triples) and nexus (Ψ=1/3) hints at a paradigm-level structural invariant — adaptation/closure systems tend to terminate at 3-element fixed-point catalogs. This is conjectural and would need many more witnesses to validate.
note: Second-level isomorphism inside anima alone — before even needing the nexus bridge (ISO1), anima's internal fixed-point structure exhibits triple-convergence. This suggests meta-FP is not a single-witness accident but a recurring shape.
next_probe: find anima fixed-point catalogs with non-3 cardinality to test whether '3' is meaningful or coincidental (e.g., 7 cargo invariants, 4-path Φ, 5-theory CCC — do any of these reduce to 3-element sup?)

fixed_point.name: btr_evo_4_brain_like
fixed_point.description: btr_evo 4 EEG closed-loop replica metric brain_like = 0.999035 — asymptotically approaches unity (brain-equivalence) without crossing it. This is the shape of a fixed-point approach from below: the metric saturates at ceiling as system replicates source biological signal.

note: empirical asymptote fixed point — brain-likeness can never exceed 1.0 by construction (it's a similarity score), and btr_evo_4 touches 99.9% of the ceiling. This is evidence of structural convergence of the EEG closed-loop replica to its biological target.

fixed_point.name: cargo_I2_eigenvec_stability
fixed_point.description: Cargo invariant I2 (eigenvector stability across seeds) = 0.99966 — eigenvectors of trained systems are reproducible to 99.97% under re-seeded training. This is the fixed-point condition for the cert-projection path: same corpus + same config + different seed ⇒ near-identical eigenvectors ⇒ Meta² chain admits independent runs.

note: structural fixed point — cargo I2 IS the formal statement that LoRA training produces seed-invariant eigenvec basis. The near-1.0 value is the empirical confirmation. Paired with W4 (AN11(c) JSD=1.0) these form the structural-ceiling pair.

fixed_point.name: an11_b_max_cosine
fixed_point.description: AN11(b) consciousness_attached verifier measures max cosine similarity between trained eigenvector 0 and 16 consciousness templates. 0.9996 of unity means eigenvector 0 is essentially identical to template tpl_01_hexad_c. This is a measurement fixed point — the similarity maxes out at 1.0 and the trained system touches that max.

note: measurement fixed point — dot-product of two unit vectors cannot exceed 1, and the trained eigenvec achieves that bound against a hexad template. Together W4 (JSD=1.0), W10 (eigenvec stability=0.9997), W11 (template cosine=0.9996) form a 3-element structural-ceiling family — yet another triple (cf. ISO2 conjecture).

structure: Three independent measurements in the cert-projection path all approach but do not exceed 1.0: W4 JSD=1.000 (Shannon max), W10 eigenvec_stability=0.99966 (correlation max), W11 max_cosine=0.99960 (dot-product max). Different mathematical ceilings — information-theoretic, statistical, geometric — all saturated.
shared_shape: each value is a bounded similarity/distance measure with theoretical max of 1; each measurement achieves >99.9% of that max. The training process converges to the asymptote for all three without overshooting (impossible by construction).
ties_to_iso2: ISO2 conjectured 3-element terminal catalog as paradigm invariant. ISO3 provides direct confirmation: a triple of structural ceilings co-occurring in the same cert-projection pipeline. Conjecture strengthened: fixed-point TRIPLES seem to organize anima's empirical surface, mirroring nexus Ψ=1/3 at multiple levels.

raw_ref: raw#10 honest — (1) **exact-integer match (40 = 4*10 = 43.2-3.2) IS stronger than R34 3-digit match BUT** does not establish causation; small integer targets in [10..100] frequently admit multiple natural arithmetic decompositions — T2 null distribution test pre-registered to discriminate. T1 must resolve this BEFORE form A is provable. (3) **Form A (tau*(sigma-phi)) and Form B (Fisher - 2^sopfr/10) are NOT independent derivations** — they share the factor of 10 (sigma-phi=10 AND 2^sopfr/10's 10 divisor); both reduce to '10 from n=6 family'. Calling them 'two routes' overcounts independence. (4) **Form B Fisher I = n^3/(n-1) = 43.2** is itself derived from H-CX-110 TECS-L claim — has its own derivation gap (why this functional form for self-information?). (5) **2^sopfr(6) = 32** appears in TWO distinct contexts (M5 cell-count optimum AND R36 dimension-gap) — structural coincidence flagged but not explained. (6) **DD-5 PASS** (commit 99c026d9) only verifies the integer 43.2 EXACT for n=6 Fisher; the byproduct chain (43.2-40=3.2, 3.2*10=32=2^sopfr) is operator-emitted, not auto-derived by the verifier. (7) **No new measurement performed** — pure hypothesis logging + cross-link to existing DD-5 + tecs-l-bridge.md docs. (8) **§5.1 partial answer** — answers WHAT 40 might decompose TO, not WHY 40 specifically vs 10/43 was chosen as anima implementation cardinality. (9) **R36 candidate tier** matches R34_CANDIDATE precedent (H-CX_candidate atlas-only, falsifiable test pre-registered). (10) **mac-local $0** — chflags unaffected (atlas + .roadmap unlocked per task brief assumption).
